title: eragrostis subglandulosa
intro: Eragrostis subglandulosa, also known as subglandulose love grass or love grass, is a species of grass native to Australia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Eragrostis subglandulosa has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antiviral and antibacterial activity. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and skin conditions.
